channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Apple Unveils $599 MacBook Neo Powered By iPhone’s A18 Pro Chip
item.content:encoded:
A new budget MacBook is entering the market.

One of Apple’s strengths is its ability to offer an entry point into its ecosystem for all family members at various price levels. Its latest tech announcement, the $599 MacBook Neo, aims to provide an affordable option for those who want to experience a MacBook without spending too much on “Pro” technology.

This new device will be available starting Wednesday, March 11, exactly one week from today.

iPhone Chip Powers the New MacBook Neo

The A18 Pro chip, launched in 2024 alongside the iPhone 16 Pro models, powers this new device. The marketing says it allows users to “breeze through their go-to apps and AI activities.”

There are two options for the brand-new Neo. The more affordable $599 models are available in Silver and a “Blush” shade of pink. The higher-priced “Citrus” green and Indigo models cost $699. The more expensive models offer 512 GB of storage instead of 256. Other than that, the tech specs are the same. Both models come with 8 GB of RAM, 2 USB-C ports, and a 3.5 mm headphone jack.

Regarding battery life, Apple claims users will get up to 16 hours of video streaming and 11 hours of wireless web browsing.

Learn more about the new Chromebook alternative from Apple at this link.

Would you be interested in picking up a MacBook Neo? Are you already in the Apple ecosystem, or would this be an entryway device for you? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Balor Games Launches as New ‘Triple I’ Publishing Label
item.content:encoded:
Good Games Group has launched the Balor Games publishing label, which focuses on producing “Triple I” games. Triple I refers to high-quality independent titles. This follows the acquisition of both the Humble Games and Firestoke catalogs.

The publishing company is led by former Humble Games leaders Alan Patmore and Mark Nash. They aim to be a curator of the highest-quality independent game titles and hope to mesh the resources and experience of a seasoned publisher with the independence that creatives want to preserve when launching indie games.

“For the developers we have worked with over the years, this moment is a reunion,” said Alan Patmore, CEO of Balor Games, in a statement. “The same leadership, and the same commitment to thoughtful publishing remain in place. What changes is our scale and our focus. Balor Games is built for inventors and backed by believers. To that end, it exists to be a seal of quality for independent games.”

More Information on Balor Games

The new label is also investing in the next generation of independent creators. It’s actively signing on to new projects, such as the co-op horror tactical shooter SCP: 5K. The label is backed by an investment from MEP Capital and plans to expand its publishing efforts while remaining selective and creating Partnerships rooted in creative alignment.

The games in the Balor catalog following the acquisitions include Slay the Spire, A Hat in Time, SIGNALIS, Coral Island, and more.

Among the games shown in the launch trailer for the label are Wildblue Skies, Threads of Time, and the aforementioned SCP: 5K. Wildblue Skies is a spiritual successor to the Star Fox franchise that many gamers feel Nintendo has forgotten about.

Insider Gaming will continue to report on any news coming from this brand-new publishing label.

How do you feel about this evolution in indie game publishing? Do you think that the new branding of “Triple I” titles will signal an important progression? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Ubisoft Confirms Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Remake Dubbed ‘Resynced’ is in Development
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has officially confirmed for the first time that the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag remake is real and will be called ‘Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced’.

The news came as part of a new blog post on the Assassin’s Creed franchise, in which Jean Guesdon, Head of Content, discussed the franchise’s progress.

First and foremost, it was confirmed that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is real, in line with past reports by Insider Gaming. The blog says that people should “keep your spyglass on the horizon”.

According to sources, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is scheduled to be revealed in mid-April, if things stay on track.

Other Assassin’s Creed Games Are in Development

The blog also confirmed that Assassin’s Creed Jade, the mobile game in partnership with Tencent, is still in development.

Assassin’s Creed Invictus, a new multiplayer title, is also still in development, but it’s said that the project “isn’t quite what the rumors have suggested.

Assassin’s Creed Hexe has also mentioned, saying, “We are taking the time to deliver on its ambitious vision, which means we’ll be quiet for a little longer, but we love seeing all the enthusiasm happening on our channels and can’t wait to unveil more when the time is right.”

Insider Gaming understands that Assassin’s Creed Hexe is one of Ubisoft’s most-anticipated titles internally, with almost everyone familiar with the project wishing they were working on it, be that because the game sounds cool or because it’s a project with seemingly the most job security.

You can read the full blog post here.

Are you looking forward to the future of the Assassin’s Creed franchise? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more gaming news, read about Sony no longer releasing single-player PlayStation exclusives on PC and PlayStation breaking the Crimson Desert console silence with a four-hour hands-on.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1720: Hints and Answer for March 5, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Wordle challenges players to guess a fresh five-letter word each day in six tries or fewer. Whether you’re a beginner or a daily player, today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1720 for March 5, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, March 5, 2026

The Wordle answer for game #1720 is another challenging puzzle. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 5 out of 6 guesses. That makes it just slightly easier than the 5.3-guess average from March 4.

General Wordle Hints For March 5

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: This word refers to a living thing, not an object or action.
  • Hint 2: It’s most often encountered in agricultural or rural contexts.
  • Hint 3: The subject it names is usually discussed as part of a group rather than individually.
  • Hint 4: You’re more likely to see this word in descriptions or metaphors than technical writing.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1720 for March 5, 2026.

Starter Words For March 5, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • SIDES
  • SHELF
  • SLEEP
  • SEEDS

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1720 On March 5, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: SHEEP

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

SHEEP is a noun that means “any of numerous ruminant mammals of the genus Ovis, of the family Bovidae, closely related to the goats, especially O. aries, bred in a number of domesticated varieties.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1719 on March 4, 2026

Answer: THEFT

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1720?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on March 6 will be Wordle Game #1721.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw
